items.title: Google to Lose High-Profile AI Staffers to Rival Anthropic
items.content: Two leading AI researchers at Google are planning to leave for rival Anthropic, according to sources, adding to a series of high-profile departures that risk undercutting the search giant’s position in AI. Bloomberg's Julia Love joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Two leading AI researchers at Google are planning to leave for rival Anthropic, according to sources, adding to a series of high-profile departures that risk undercutting the search giant’s position in AI. Bloomberg's Julia Love joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Waymo Registers German Entity for Future European Expansion
items.content: Waymo is planning to offer its driverless robotaxis in Germany, the latest move by the Alphabet Inc. unit to expand outside the US.
items.contentSnippet: Waymo is planning to offer its driverless robotaxis in Germany, the latest move by the Alphabet Inc. unit to expand outside the US.
items.creator: Natalie Lung

items.title: AI Sales Start to Justify Data-Center Spending Boom, Report Says
items.content: Revenue from AI has reached a tipping point, showing that the hundreds of billions of dollars tech companies are spending on it may be economically sustainable, according to a report from research firm Exponential View. Azeem Azhar, founder of Exponential View, joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech" to discuss the findings of this report. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Revenue from AI has reached a tipping point, showing that the hundreds of billions of dollars tech companies are spending on it may be economically sustainable, according to a report from research firm Exponential View. Azeem Azhar, founder of Exponential View, joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech" to discuss the findings of this report. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Qualcomm CEO on Data Center Chips, Adding Meta as a Customer
items.content: Qualcomm says its expansion into chips for data centers will produce “billions” in revenue in the fiscal year that begins in October, suggesting the company is gaining a foothold in the booming market for AI gear. Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon joins Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow and Romaine Bostick on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Qualcomm says its expansion into chips for data centers will produce “billions” in revenue in the fiscal year that begins in October, suggesting the company is gaining a foothold in the booming market for AI gear. Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon joins Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow and Romaine Bostick on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Apple Raises Prices, Micron Rallies | Bloomberg Tech 6/25/2026
items.content:
Bloomberg’s Ed Ludlow breaks down Micron's blowout AI-fueled forecast shattering Wall Street's projections, and Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon joins to weigh in on the company's own earnings and strong annual sales forecast. Plus, Apple raises prices on some hardware, an extreme measure in direct response to the memory and storage crunch.

Correction: This video has been edited with an updated graphic which reflects the new price of the iPad Pro is $1,199. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet:
Bloomberg’s Ed Ludlow breaks down Micron's blowout AI-fueled forecast shattering Wall Street's projections, and Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon joins to weigh in on the company's own earnings and strong annual sales forecast. Plus, Apple raises prices on some hardware, an extreme measure in direct response to the memory and storage crunch.

Correction: This video has been edited with an updated graphic which reflects the new price of the iPad Pro is $1,199. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Apple's Sweeping Price Hikes Hit iPads and Macs
items.content: Apple shares fell after it raised prices of all Macs, iPads, home devices and the Vision Pro on Thursday, seeking to offset cost hikes caused by an unprecedented shortage of memory chips and storage. Ed Ludlow explains.  (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Apple shares fell after it raised prices of all Macs, iPads, home devices and the Vision Pro on Thursday, seeking to offset cost hikes caused by an unprecedented shortage of memory chips and storage. Ed Ludlow explains.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Apple Hikes Prices to Counter Memory Shortages
items.content:
Apple raised prices of Macs, iPads, home devices, and the Vision Pro to offset cost hikes caused by a shortage of memory chips and storage. Bloomberg's consumer tech lead Mark Gurman joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech."

Correction: This video has been edited with an updated graphic which reflects the new price of the iPad Pro is $1,199. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet:
Apple raised prices of Macs, iPads, home devices, and the Vision Pro to offset cost hikes caused by a shortage of memory chips and storage. Bloomberg's consumer tech lead Mark Gurman joins Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech."

Correction: This video has been edited with an updated graphic which reflects the new price of the iPad Pro is $1,199. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: H.B. Fuller Agrees to Acquire UK-Listed AMS for £659 Million
items.content: H.B. Fuller Co. agreed to acquire Advanced Medical Solutions Group Plc, a British maker of tissue-healing medical products, for £659 million ($870 million).
items.contentSnippet: H.B. Fuller Co. agreed to acquire Advanced Medical Solutions Group Plc, a British maker of tissue-healing medical products, for £659 million ($870 million).
items.creator: Liana Baker, Mengqi Sun

items.title: Microsoft Raises Xbox Console Prices for Third Time in 13 Months
items.content: Microsoft Corp. announced a third substantial price increase for the company’s current-generation Xbox video-game consoles in a glaring example of the component shortage crisis that has universally driven up the cost of consumer tech products.
items.contentSnippet: Microsoft Corp. announced a third substantial price increase for the company’s current-generation Xbox video-game consoles in a glaring example of the component shortage crisis that has universally driven up the cost of consumer tech products.
items.creator: Chris Welch

items.title: Apple Raises Prices on Some Products by $500
items.content: Apple customers may experience sticker shock next time they look to buy one of many products. The company is raising prices of Macs, iPads, home devices, and the Vision Pro to offset cost hikes caused by a shortage of memory chips and storage. Prices for iPhone, Apple Watch and AirPods stay the same. Mark Gurman reports. (This video has been edited with an updated graphic which reflects the new price of the iPad Pro is $1,199.) (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Apple customers may experience sticker shock next time they look to buy one of many products. The company is raising prices of Macs, iPads, home devices, and the Vision Pro to offset cost hikes caused by a shortage of memory chips and storage. Prices for iPhone, Apple Watch and AirPods stay the same. Mark Gurman reports. (This video has been edited with an updated graphic which reflects the new price of the iPad Pro is $1,199.) (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Bob Sloan's Market Positioning Warning
items.content: Markets may be near record highs, but Bob Sloan, S3 Partners Founder & Managing Partner says the real story is happening beneath the surface. He joins Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why passive investors are fueling AI stocks higher while hedge funds quietly sell into the rally and why tracking investor positioning may be just as important as earnings or valuations. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Markets may be near record highs, but Bob Sloan, S3 Partners Founder & Managing Partner says the real story is happening beneath the surface. He joins Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why passive investors are fueling AI stocks higher while hedge funds quietly sell into the rally and why tracking investor positioning may be just as important as earnings or valuations. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Sonos Cuts 3% of Staff Across User Experience, Product Teams
items.content: Sonos Inc. made cuts across its user experience, product and design teams, citing a need to streamline the organization and operate more efficiently.
items.contentSnippet: Sonos Inc. made cuts across its user experience, product and design teams, citing a need to streamline the organization and operate more efficiently.
items.creator: Chris Welch

items.title: Micron's AI Pricing Power Explained
items.content: Micron's pricing power may have permanently changed the semiconductor industry. D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why AI has turned memory into a strategic asset and how Apple could actually emerge stronger despite rising chip costs. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Micron's pricing power may have permanently changed the semiconductor industry. D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why AI has turned memory into a strategic asset and how Apple could actually emerge stronger despite rising chip costs. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Apple to Skip High-End M6 Mac Chips in Favor of AI-Focused M7 Line
items.content: Apple Inc. is making one of the biggest-ever changes to its Mac silicon strategy, preparing to jump ahead to a new artificial intelligence-focused chip generation for its next top-end processors.
items.contentSnippet: Apple Inc. is making one of the biggest-ever changes to its Mac silicon strategy, preparing to jump ahead to a new artificial intelligence-focused chip generation for its next top-end processors.
items.creator: Mark Gurman

items.title: Apple Shares Sink After Sweeping Price Hikes Hit iPads and Macs
items.content: Apple Inc. shares fell after it raised prices of all Macs, iPads, home devices and the Vision Pro on Thursday, seeking to offset cost hikes caused by an unprecedented shortage of memory chips and storage.
items.contentSnippet: Apple Inc. shares fell after it raised prices of all Macs, iPads, home devices and the Vision Pro on Thursday, seeking to offset cost hikes caused by an unprecedented shortage of memory chips and storage.

items.title: Chipmakers Rally With Traders Piling In After Micron Results
items.content: Semiconductor stocks surged on Thursday as Micron Technology Inc.’s strong results underscored how artificial intelligence remains a major vector for growth.
items.contentSnippet: Semiconductor stocks surged on Thursday as Micron Technology Inc.’s strong results underscored how artificial intelligence remains a major vector for growth.
items.creator: Ryan Vlastelica, Subrat Patnaik

items.title: IBM Rallies on New Chip; McCormick Moves on Guidance | Stock Movers
items.content:
On this episode of Stock Movers:

- IBM (IBM) shares gained in premarket trading on Thursday after the technology giant unveiled the world’s first sub-1 nanometer (nm) chip technology.
- McCormick (MKC) is moving after it reported second-quarter profit that beat estimates, buoyed by higher prices and a tariff refund, and reaffirmed its full-year guidance.
- Darden Restaurants (DRI) shares fell after its same-store sales at Olive Garden trailed expectations, raising questions about demand at the company’s largest chain and overshadowing better-than-expected earnings. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet:
On this episode of Stock Movers:

- IBM (IBM) shares gained in premarket trading on Thursday after the technology giant unveiled the world’s first sub-1 nanometer (nm) chip technology.
- McCormick (MKC) is moving after it reported second-quarter profit that beat estimates, buoyed by higher prices and a tariff refund, and reaffirmed its full-year guidance.
- Darden Restaurants (DRI) shares fell after its same-store sales at Olive Garden trailed expectations, raising questions about demand at the company’s largest chain and overshadowing better-than-expected earnings. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: DeepSeek Plans to Double Staff Across All Departments
items.content: China’s DeepSeek, fresh off agreements for a blockbuster fundraising, said it is working to at least double the size of all departments, as it steps up efforts to compete with domestic rivals and global leaders such as OpenAI and Anthropic PBC.
items.contentSnippet: China’s DeepSeek, fresh off agreements for a blockbuster fundraising, said it is working to at least double the size of all departments, as it steps up efforts to compete with domestic rivals and global leaders such as OpenAI and Anthropic PBC.
items.creator: Felix Tam

items.title: Trump Sons Invested in Private Fund Making Millions on Microcaps
items.content: A fund that includes President Donald Trump’s eldest sons among its investors has booked hundreds of millions of dollars in paper profits through a lucrative playbook that involves taking stakes in a steady stream of small, thinly traded public companies.
items.contentSnippet: A fund that includes President Donald Trump’s eldest sons among its investors has booked hundreds of millions of dollars in paper profits through a lucrative playbook that involves taking stakes in a steady stream of small, thinly traded public companies.
items.creator: Olga Kharif, Annie Massa

items.title: Data Center Surge Brings Risk for States and Munis, Moody’s Says
items.content: A flood of data center projects with their heavy power and water demands are introducing credit risks for state and local governments, according to Moody’s Ratings.
items.contentSnippet: A flood of data center projects with their heavy power and water demands are introducing credit risks for state and local governments, according to Moody’s Ratings.
items.creator: Shruti Singh, Nic Querolo

items.title: Nvidia Wants to Make Humanoid AI Robots Safer Around Humans
items.content: Humanoid robots are getting smarter and moving closer to the real world. But before they can work alongside humans, Nvidia says they need something even more important: the ability to recognize danger and react in an instant. Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow speaks with Deepu Talla, Vice President of Robotics and Edge AI at Nvidia, about the technology designed to make that possible. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Humanoid robots are getting smarter and moving closer to the real world. Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow speaks with Deepu Talla, Vice President of Robotics and Edge AI at Nvidia, about the technology designed to make that possible. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Cerebras On Track for Record Two-Day Loss as Outlook Disappoints
items.content: Cerebras Systems Inc. shares tumbled Thursday — and were on track for a record two-session loss — after the chipmaker gave a disappointing annual sales outlook.
items.contentSnippet: Cerebras Systems Inc. shares tumbled Thursday — and were on track for a record two-session loss — after the chipmaker gave a disappointing annual sales outlook.
items.creator: Carmen Reinicke

items.title: Qualcomm Jumps on $15 billion Data Center Sales Projection
items.content: Qualcomm Inc. shares jumped after the chipmaker forecast annual sales of more than $15 billion from artificial intelligence components in data centers by fiscal 2029.
items.contentSnippet: Qualcomm Inc. shares jumped after the chipmaker forecast annual sales of more than $15 billion from artificial intelligence components in data centers by fiscal 2029.
items.creator: Ian King

items.title: Samsung Joins With US Battery Startup Seeking Pentagon Cash
items.content: Samsung SDI Co. has agreed to provide $20 million and engineering support to Forge Nano Inc., a US battery startup aiming to build cells for the US defense and aerospace industries at a new plant in North Carolina.
items.contentSnippet: Samsung SDI Co. has agreed to provide $20 million and engineering support to Forge Nano Inc., a US battery startup aiming to build cells for the US defense and aerospace industries at a new plant in North Carolina.
items.creator: Gabrielle Coppola

items.title: SK Hynix, Micron Solidify the Memory Chip as Runaway Star of AI
items.content: With back-to-back announcements this week, SK Hynix Inc. and Micron Technology Inc. have solidified the memory chip market as the hottest part of the AI industry.
items.contentSnippet: With back-to-back announcements this week, SK Hynix Inc. and Micron Technology Inc. have solidified the memory chip market as the hottest part of the AI industry.
items.creator: Dina Bass, Nick Turner

items.title: Micron Shares Soar After AI-Fueled Forecast Shatters Projections
items.content: Micron Technology Inc. shares surged after the largest US maker of computer memory chips issued a quarterly sales forecast that crushed Wall Street estimates, signaling that an AI-fueled growth run remains strong.
items.contentSnippet: Micron Technology Inc. shares surged after the largest US maker of computer memory chips issued a quarterly sales forecast that crushed Wall Street estimates, signaling that an AI-fueled growth run remains strong.
items.creator: Dina Bass

items.title: Apple Raises Prices on Macs, iPads, Home Devices
items.content: Apple is raising prices on several products to offset cost hikes caused by a shortage of memory chips and storage. Prices are going up for the MacBook Neo, MacBook Pro, MacBook Air, iPad Air and iPad Pro. Price hikes are global and vary by product.  (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Apple is raising prices on several products to offset cost hikes caused by a shortage of memory chips and storage. Prices are going up for the MacBook Neo, MacBook Pro, MacBook Air, iPad Air and iPad Pro. Price hikes are global and vary by product.  (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Bloomberg Tech San Francisco 2026 Highlights
items.content: Led by Bloomberg's Emily Chang and Tom Giles, Bloomberg Tech 2026 brought together the leading CEOs, investors, and innovators who are harnessing technology to change the world around us. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.contentSnippet: Led by Bloomberg's Emily Chang and Tom Giles, Bloomberg Tech 2026 brought together the leading CEOs, investors, and innovators who are harnessing technology to change the world around us. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: AI Cloud Provider IREN Gets Logo on Golden State Warriors Jerseys
items.content: The latest sign of the AI data center boom going mainstream: IREN Ltd., an Australia-founded cloud computing provider, will have its logo featured on the jerseys of the Golden State Warriors starting next season.
items.contentSnippet: The latest sign of the AI data center boom going mainstream: IREN Ltd., an Australia-founded cloud computing provider, will have its logo featured on the jerseys of the Golden State Warriors starting next season.
items.creator: Dina Bass

items.title: For $4,500 a Week, AI Can Tutor Your Kids in the Hamptons
items.content: Artificial intelligence is coming to summer school in the Hamptons this year, with tech-powered programs teaching academics alongside lessons in how to prepare a gourmet meal and sell a luxury home.
items.contentSnippet: Artificial intelligence is coming to summer school in the Hamptons this year, with tech-powered programs teaching academics alongside lessons in how to prepare a gourmet meal and sell a luxury home.
